# .strictcli/ directory with auto-generated CLI schema

## Context

strictcli has 10 consumer projects (6 Python, 4 Go). There's no standard way to discover consumers or inspect their CLI structure without reading source code. selfdoc currently uses fragile AST-based extraction with separate Python and Go parsers.

## Problem

- No way to discover strictcli consumers besides grepping dependency files
- selfdoc's AST extraction is fragile and language-specific
- No authoritative cross-language CLI schema format
- Shell completion generators, CI tools, and documentation tools have no machine-readable source

## Proposed solution

### .strictcli/schema.json

A runtime-generated JSON file describing the full CLI structure: commands, groups, flags (name, type, short, default, choices, repeatable, env, help), args (name, required, default, variadic, help), tags, mutex groups, dependencies, passthrough commands, deprecated commands.

Generated by strictcli itself via an auto-injected `--dump-schema` flag on every App. Both Python and Go implementations produce the same format.

### .strictcli/config.json

Static metadata: language (python/go), strictcli version, entry point, app name.

### Auto-generation via pre-release hook

Each consumer project's `.rlsbl/hooks/pre-release.sh` runs the app with `--dump-schema` to regenerate `.strictcli/schema.json`. The resulting commit uses the `Autogenerated: true` trailer (via `rlsbl commit` or `safegit commit --trailer`), so it's automatically exempted from changelog coverage. The schema stays current without manual effort or changelog noise.

## Work items

1. Design the schema.json format (shared between Python and Go)
2. Add `--dump-schema` flag to Python App (auto-injected, writes .strictcli/schema.json)
3. Add `--dump-schema` flag to Go App (same behavior, same format)
4. Add schema.json generation to conformance suite (verify both implementations produce identical schemas for the same app definition)
5. Create .strictcli/config.json format
6. Roll out to all 10 consumer projects:
   - Python: rlsbl, claudewheel, predraw, selfdoc, ClaudeTimeline, claudestream
   - Go: safegit, howmuchleft, migrable, saferm
7. Add pre-release hook to each consumer's .rlsbl/hooks/pre-release.sh

## Depends on

Nothing -- can be built independently. selfdoc migration to read schema.json (filed separately in selfdoc/todo/) happens after this stabilizes.

## Effort

Medium. Schema format design is the hardest part (must be complete enough for selfdoc and shell completions). Implementation in Python and Go is straightforward (App already knows its own structure). Rollout is mechanical.
